arguments.task: Research Attio using official first-party sources only. Open Attio's developer/API docs, authentication docs, pricing page, and any official rate limit docs. Also check official GitHub or MCP registry pages for an Attio MCP/server if linked from official sources or clearly named public registry entries. Extract: one-line product description, API auth methods, whether access is self-serve free/trial/paid/gated, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), breadth of API, rate limit notes, notable signals, and URLs supporting each fact. Avoid blogs unless an official page is unavailable.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Attio sources and supporting registry pages.

Authentication Methods:**\\n- **OAuth 2.0:** Recommended for third-party integrations and the primary method for the MCP server.\\n- **API Keys:** Supported for internal/private scripts via Bearer tokens or HTTP Basic Auth.\\n- **URL:** [https://docs.attio.com/rest-api/authenticating-requests](https://docs.attio.com/rest-api/authenticating-requests)\\n\\n**2. Pricing and Access:**\\n- **Self-Serve Access:** Yes, users can 'Start for free' without a credit card.\\n- **Tiers:**\\n    - **Free:** $0 (up to 3 seats, 50k records, basic syncing).\\n    - **Plus:** $29/user/mo billed annually (private lists, 250k records).\\n    - **Pro:** $69/user/mo billed annually (Advanced permissions, 1M records, priority support).\\n    - **Enterprise:** Custom pricing (Unlimited objects, SSO, SCIM, dedicated support).\\n- **URL:** [https://attio.com/pricing](https://attio.com/...[truncated]

raw_result.description: Data-driven CRM for modern teams with customizable objects, syncing, and automation.
raw_result.auth_notes: Attio documents OAuth 2.0 for third-party integrations and API keys for internal use; API keys can be sent as Bearer tokens or via HTTP Basic auth. The hosted MCP server uses OAuth.
raw_result.gating_reason: Attio offers a self-serve Free plan with 'Start for free' signup and public API documentation; higher tiers add capabilities like SQL/SCIM but basic platform access is not gated.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with broad CRM coverage across objects, attributes, records, lists, entries, workspace members, notes, tasks, threads, comments, meetings, call recordings, files, webhooks, plus documented SQL and SCIM surfaces for higher tiers.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Attio publishes an official hosted MCP server at mcp.attio.com with first-party docs describing OAuth connection and AI client availability.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: REST API docs state 100 read requests/sec and 25 write requests/sec per workspace. Lists/list entries also use score-based limits (typically 1,000 points/minute). MCP docs note additional limits such as search at 300/minute and semantic search/reporting at 2/sec.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official first-party hosted MCP server; public developer docs; self-serve free tier; API docs emphasize OAuth and agent connectivity. Enterprise-tier signals include SCIM/SSO and SQL access.
